title: Storyboard · "Knock" — Radio→Animation production script · S18
body:
**Scene 18: 22:30–24:00 · Walter Opens the Door**

HERO FRAME: Walter (from behind, silhouetted) standing in his open doorway · a soft warm light from the hallway outside · the SHAPE of a figure on the threshold but we do not see the figure clearly — it is implied through soft outline only · the visitor wears something like a long coat, holds something like a hat, has something like a kind face but ALL OF IT IS SUGGESTED

MOTION: kling — the warm light from the hallway brightens slowly, washing over Walter's silhouette. The implied figure on the threshold seems to step very slightly forward. Walter's shoulders, which have been tense for the entire episode, drop slightly for the first time.

MOOD: **Revelation.** Played with restraint, never spectacle.

AUDIO: A long silence. Then the small click of the latch. The door opens.

The Visitor's voice, kind and unhurried: *"Hello, Walter. I have been waiting to find you."*

source.label: Dimension X episode #05 (NBC Radio, May 6, 1950) adapting Fredric Brown's 1948 short-short story "Knock" (originally in *Thrilling Wonder Stories*, December 1948)

author: matt

witnesses.label: Rufinus of Aquileia, Commentary on the Apostles' Creed (c. AD 404)
Pirminius, De singulis libris canonicis scarapsus (c. AD 750) — earliest received form
Cited by every major catechism since 8th century
